title: User ID not stored in PostgreSQL Chat Memory
symptom: The PostgreSQL Chat Memory node does not store the userId even though it is present in the incoming JSON payload.
applies_when: Sending userId in the JSON payload
not_this_if: User ID is stored correctly in other nodes
root_cause: The PostgreSQL Chat Memory node is primarily designed to store chat history and does not support additional fields like userId directly. This limitation prevents the userId from being mapped and stored in the database.
fix_steps: Add a PostgreSQL Update node after the Chat Memory node.
Configure the Update node to set the userId in the appropriate column of your chat_histories table.
agent_instruction: Use a PostgreSQL Update node to store the userId after the Chat Memory node processes the chat message.
